title: Joint rehabilitation apparatus
abstract: A rehabilitation apparatus (10) for facilitating joint movement in a patient having a limb with a joint, apparatus (10) including actuation member (12) and actuation strap (14) extending from actuation member (12), actuation member (12) selectively retracting actuation strap (14), actuation strap (14) having strap end (16) engageable with the limb of the patient. Security strap (18) is connected to actuation member (12). When the limb of the patient is engaged with strap end (16) of actuation strap (14) and actuation member (12) retracts actuation strap (14), actuation strap (14) produces a force on the limb. Security strap (18) can be a harness receivable about patient's upper torso (50). Apparatus (10) can further include brace (64), the joint of the patient's limb receivable in brace (64), actuation strap (14) engageable with brace (64), actuation strap (14) producing a force on the limb via brace (64).
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . A rehabilitation apparatus for facilitating joint movement in a patient having a limb with a joint, the apparatus comprising: an actuation member; an actuation strap extending from the actuation member, the actuation member selectively retracting the actuation strap, the actuation strap having a strap end engageable with the limb of the patient; a security strap connected to the actuation member; and a brace, the joint of the patient's limb receivable in the brace, the actuation strap positionable on the brace when the patient's joint is received in the brace and the patient's limb is received in the strap end of the actuation strap, wherein when the joint of the patient is received in the brace, the limb of the patient is engaged with the strap end of the actuation strap, the actuation strap is positioned on the brace, and the actuation member retracts the actuation strap, the actuation strap produces a force on the limb via the brace. 2. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the force on the patient's limb biases the limb to move at the joint. 3. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the patient has an upper torso, and the security strap is a harness receivable about the upper torso of the patient. 4. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the actuation member includes a pawl and ratchet system. 5. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein: the actuation member has a collection area and a spool located in the collection area; and the actuation strap winds around the spool as the actuation member selectively retracts the actuation strap. 6. The apparatus of Claim 5, wherein the actuation member includes an outer housing, and the collection area is at least partially defined within the outer housing. 7. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the force produced on the limb via the brace biases the limb toward a straightened position. 8. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein: the brace further comprises an extension member extending from the brace in an outward direction from the limb when the joint of the patient is received in the brace; and the actuation strap is positionable on the extension member when the patient's joint is received in the brace and the patient's limb is received by the strap end of the actuation strap. 9. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein: the patient's limb is a leg including a knee joint and a foot; the knee joint is receivable in the brace; the foot is receivable in the strap end of the actuation strap; and the actuation strap produces a downward force on the knee joint via the brace when the actuation member retracts the actuation strap, the downward force biasing the patient's leg toward a straightened position. 10. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the brace includes one or more pads positionable about the joint of the patient when the joint is received in the brace, each pad including a raised central portion. 1 1. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the actuation strap further includes a plurality of guide markings. 12. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the actuation member further comprises a release mechanism, the actuation strap extendable from the actuation member when the release mechanism is engaged. 13. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the limb is a leg of the patient, the leg including a foot, and the apparatus further comprises: a foot device securable to the foot of the patient, the foot device having a first fastener member extending in a laterally inward direction from the foot device, a second fastener member extending in a laterally outward direction from the foot device, and a third fastener member extending in a longitudinally outward direction from the foot device; wherein the actuation strap is engageable with the first, second, or third fastener members on the foot device when the foot device is secured to the foot of the patient. 14. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein the strap end of the actuation strap includes a loop sized to receive at least a portion of the patient's limb. 15. The apparatus of Claim 1 , wherein: the brace further comprises an extension member; the extension member is engageable with the actuation strap at a point on the actuation strap between the strap end and the actuation member when the joint is received in the brace and the limb is received in the strap end; the extension member produces an angle between 0 and 180 degrees in the actuation strap as the actuation member retracts the actuation strap; and the actuation strap produces a force on the limb via the extension member of the brace when the actuation member retracts the actuation strap, the force biasing the limb towards a straightened position.
